---
title: "RNA-seq Quality Control Report"
date: "`r Sys.Date()`"
format:
  html:
    toc: true
    code-fold: true
    theme: cosmo
    embed-resources: true
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = FALSE, warning = FALSE, message = FALSE)
# Only base + tidyverse packages are used so this renders in the standard
# rocker/verse container without installing extra dependencies.
library(ggplot2)
library(dplyr)
library(tidyr)
library(jsonlite)
```

# Introduction

This report provides a summary of the quality control steps for the RNA-seq analysis pipeline.

## Data Source
Parsing MultiQC JSON output.

```{r load_data}
# Look for multiqc_data.json in the current directory or a 'multiqc_data' subdirectory
json_file <- list.files(pattern = "multiqc_data.json", recursive = TRUE, full.names = TRUE)[1]

if (is.na(json_file)) {
  stop("Could not find multiqc_data.json file.")
}

mqc_data <- fromJSON(json_file)
```

# General Statistics

```{r general_stats}
# Extract General Stats
# Structure depends on MultiQC version, but usually under report_general_stats_data
stats_list <- mqc_data$report_general_stats_data
# Combine list of lists into a dataframe
stats_df <- bind_rows(stats_list, .id = "Sample")

# Depending on exact structure, we might need to handle specific columns
if (!is.null(stats_df)) {
  # Clean up column names if needed
  knitr::kable(stats_df, caption = "General Statistics Summary")
} else {
  print("No general statistics found in JSON.")
}
```

# Read Counts (Fastp)

```{r fastp_counts}
# Check for Fastp data
if ("fastp" %in% names(mqc_data$report_saved_raw_data)) {
  fastp_raw <- mqc_data$report_saved_raw_data$fastp
  # Depending on structure, might be nested. 
  # Usually multiqc saves nice tables. Let's try to find the plot data specifically or just parse raw.
  
  # Simplification: Create a dummy plot if parsing fails or just showing concepts
  # Ideally we parse `multiqc_general_stats`
  
  if ("fastp_mqc-generalstats-fastp-filtering_result_passed_filter_reads" %in% names(stats_df)) {
      plot_df <- stats_df %>%
        select(Sample, Passed = `fastp_mqc-generalstats-fastp-filtering_result_passed_filter_reads`) %>%
        mutate(Passed = as.numeric(Passed))
      
      p <- ggplot(plot_df, aes(x = Sample, y = Passed)) +
        geom_bar(stat = "identity", fill = "steelblue") +
        theme_minimal() +
        coord_flip() +
        labs(title = "Reads Passing Filter (Fastp)", y = "Count")

      print(p)
  }
}
```

# FastQC Summary

```{r fastqc_summary}
# FastQC usually contributes to general stats too
qc_cols <- grep("FastQC", names(stats_df), value = TRUE, ignore.case = TRUE)

if (length(qc_cols) > 0) {
  # visualize first metric found
  col_to_plot <- qc_cols[1]
  
  p2 <- ggplot(stats_df, aes(x = .data[["Sample"]], y = .data[[col_to_plot]])) +
    geom_bar(stat = "identity", fill = "salmon") +
    theme_minimal() +
    coord_flip() +
    labs(title = paste("FastQC:", col_to_plot), x = "Sample")

  print(p2)
}
```
